content.Text: I’m checking the existing Flutter screen and service patterns first so the new feature matches the app’s structure, tab handling, and persistence conventions.
`rg` is not installed here, so I’m falling back to targeted reads with PowerShell. I’m pulling a few comparable screens and the persistence utility before I lay out the new service and4-tab screen.
I have the baseline patterns now. Next I’m checking a couple of tracker screens that persist UI state so the coach screen saves dismissed nudges the same way the rest of the app does.

ToolUse.name: Run Select-String -Path lib\views\home\*.dart -Pattern "ScreenPersistence<|loadMap\(|saveMap\(|TabController\(length:" | Select-Object -First 120 | ForEach-Object { $_.Path + ':' + $_.LineNumber + ': ' + $_.Line.Trim() }
ToolUse.raw_input: {"call_id":"call_4uAJQv1fVAZaDDZDDDNVt6z9","turn_id":"019da627-4c52-7711-9aa6-b6885fcf9b0c","command":["C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe","-Command","Select-String -Path lib\\views\\home\\*.dart -Pattern \"ScreenPersistence<|loadMap\\(|saveMap\\(|TabController\\(length:\" | Select-Object -First 120 | ForEach-Object { $_.Path + ':' + $_.LineNumber + ': ' + $_.Line.Trim() }"],"cwd":"C:\\Users\\onlin\\.openclaw\\workspace\\temp-builder","parsed_cmd":[{"type":"unknown","cmd":"Select-String -Path lib\\views\\home\\*.dart -Pattern \"ScreenPersistence<|loadMap\\(|saveMap\\(|TabController\\(length:\" | Select-Object -First 120 | ForEach-Object { $_.Path + ':' + $_.LineNumber + ': ' + $_.Line.Trim() }"}],"source":"agent"}
